business.headline: After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India in global stock market rankingsSouth Korea has moved past India in global stock market rankings after a sharp rally in AI-linked chipmakers. The shift underlines how investors are favouring markets tied to the AI supply chain while Indian equities face near-term pressures.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,300; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 302.16 points, or 0.41%, to 73,965.18 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 107.05 points, or 0.46%, to 23,275.55 as of 9:25 am.

Trump cuts tariffs on farm, industrial equipment from 25% to 15%. What it meansUS President Donald Trump has lowered US tariffs on selected farm and industrial equipment from 25% to 15% through December 2027. The move couples cheaper imports with incentives for greater use of American steel and aluminium.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

India may reconsider Scotch whisky tariff cuts if UK imposes steel curbsIndia could revisit tariff concessions granted to Britain on products such as Scotch whisky if London fails to address New Delhi's concerns over proposed steel restrictions, according to an Indian official.

Can small-cap funds really deliver higher long-term returns?While small caps have delivered better returns than large caps over the past two decades, the difference may be smaller than many investors expect, says a recent study.

SIP investing, the RCB way18 years. That's how long RCB supporters waited for glory. If patience eventually paid off in cricket, could the same principle be the secret behind successful SIP investing?

What is Section 301 of the US Trade Act? Why India wants protection from itAs negotiations enter the final stage, one issue attracting attention is India's effort to secure protection from possible US tariffs under Section 301 of the US Trade Act, 1974.

Piyush Goyal says 99% of India-US trade deal talks completedSpeaking about the ongoing negotiations, Goyal said discussions between the two sides would continue from June 2 to June 4 to resolve a few remaining issues before the agreement is formally announced.

Sensex sheds 500 points, Nifty slips below 23,400 despite IT rallyThe BSE Sensex fell 508.40 points, or 0.68%, to close at 74,267.34, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 165.15 points, or 0.70%, to settle at 23,382.60.

LPG prices, solar rules, tax and more : 5 big financial changes this JuneFrom LPG cylinder prices to tax payments, June brings a fresh set of financial changes for households and businesses. Some may increase costs, while others are aimed at improving security and supporting local manufacturing.

India tightens solar import rules from June 1. Here's what it means for consumersThe government now requires certain solar projects to use only domestically manufactured solar cells, a move aimed at reducing dependence on Chinese imports and strengthening local manufacturing.

US tightens curbs on Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms operating overseasThe US' best AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places such as Malaysia despite broader US efforts to starve Chinese firms of semiconductors needed to develop critical AI capabilities, reported Reuters.

Have retail investors become the real gatekeepers of India's IPO market?The broader shift in India's equity markets from one that looked to foreign capital for validation to one anchored in domestic conviction has reached the primary market fully.

India, US begin latest round of talks to finalise details of interim trade pactThe visit, scheduled from June 1 to 4 in New Delhi, will follow recent diplomatic mobility between India and the US and build on the Indian delegation's visit to Washington in April this year.
IndiGo shares rise despite Q4 loss. What's driving the rally?IndiGo shares rose even after the airline posted a March-quarter loss. Here's why the aviation company's stock price is rising today.

Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS jump up to 5%; why are IT stocks rallying today?US rate-cut expectations and hopes of a rebound in technology spending are driving fresh interest in IT stocks, helping the sector outperform the broader market after months of lagging.

Sensex gains nearly 300 points, Nifty above 23,600; IndiGo jumps 5%The BSE Sensex was up 312.44 points, or 0.42%, at 75,088.18 at 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 86.20 points, or 0.37%, to 23,633.95.

Can a 10-year SIP really make you rich? Here's the truth you need to knowInvest a few thousand rupees every month, stay invested for 10 years, and become rich, that is the promise often attached to mutual fund SIPs. But is wealth creation really that simple, or is there more to the story?

Commercial LPG cylinders become costlier from today, Delhi price up by Rs 42Commercial LPG cylinder prices were raised in Delhi and Kolkata from June 1. The move came alongside the government's push for stronger reserves and tougher anti-hoarding checks. However, there has been no change in the price of domestic LPG cylinders used by households.

entertainment.headline: Kangana recalls mother's reaction to Gangster: Society won't accept this Kangana Ranaut said her parents were unimpressed after watching her debut film Gangster and focused on society's reaction to her role. She said their views changed after her National Award recognition.

No India-Pak mission in Alpha, Alia Bhatt's film is an assassin origin story Alia Bhatt's Alpha is being positioned as an assassin origin story in the YRF Spy Universe, not an India-Pakistan joint mission. The shift moves the film away from rumours about a possible mission involving two countries and towards a harder action-led narrative.

Vignesh Shivan to Lokesh Kanagaraj: Why are filmmakers blaming audience for flops? Vignesh Shivan's post on Love Insurance Kompany has renewed debate over filmmakers blaming audiences and online negativity after flops. The backlash has drawn parallels with comments by Lokesh Kanagaraj and others, sharpening questions over whether films simply failed to connect.

It's a Toy Story, and Taylor Swift said yes: Singer announces new song Taylor Swift has announced an original song for Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5. Titled I Knew It, I Knew You, the track will release on June 5.

Was like a kept boyfriend: Lalit Modi defends Sushmita Sen over gold digger tag Lalit Modi has defended Sushmita Sen against the 'gold digger' label that followed their 2022 relationship reveal. He said she was self-made and never depended on him.

The End of Oak Street trailer: Anne Hathaway trapped in prehistoric world gone wild The trailer for The End of Oak Street follows Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor as a suburban family whose neighbourhood is suddenly transported to a mysterious land filled with dinosaurs. After a strange cosmic event, they are forced to survive in an unfamiliar and dangerous world.

Producer TP Aggarwal approaches court against film body's stand on Ranveer Singh Producer TP Aggarwal has approached the Bombay Civil Court against FWICE and IMPPA, saying no organisation has the authority to ban members of the film industry. His petition comes in the wake of FWICE's recent action against Ranveer Singh in connection with Don 3.

Bigg Boss Tamil 10: Vijay Sethupathi welcomes common man to new season The promo for Bigg Boss Tamil 10 featuring Vijay Sethupathi has been unveiled. It introduces the new Common Man concept, a shift from celebrity-led casting that has heightened viewer curiosity as the show prepares for its next season.

Priyanka Mohan is beyond grateful after appointment as Korea Tourism Ambassador They Call Him OG actor Priyanka Mohan has been appointed Honorary Ambassador for Korea Tourism, marking another milestone in her growing association with South Korea and its culture.

Rohit Shetty teases raw and real stunts in new Khatron Ke Khiladi 15 promo Rohit Shetty has shared a new promo for Khatron Ke Khiladi 15, offering a glimpse of the high-octane stunts planned for the new season. The filmmaker also promised viewers a thrilling ride packed with "raw and real" action.

Madhuri Dixit on life after marriage: Was no more the centre of attention Madhuri Dixit opened up about moving to the US after marrying Dr Shriram Nene and how motherhood became the focus of her life. The actor also spoke about taking on different roles over the years and why she has never been afraid to try something new.

Sanjay Gupta slams remix trend after Ucha Lamba Kad Forever, Chunari Chunari release Sanjay Gupta has questioned Bollywood's rush to repackage old hit songs after fresh remixes of Chunnari Chunnari and Ucha Lamba Kad were released.

Trisha can't get over her 'magical' May, shares new recap after CM Vijay's oath Trisha Krishnan shared a new "magical May" photo dump, reflecting on the month she turned 43 and made several cherished memories. The post arrived days after her appearance at CM Vijay's oath-taking ceremony and her widely discussed birthday visit to his Chennai home.

Influencer Dharna Durga has a message for those questioning her Maa Behen role Influencer Dharna Durga said she is ignoring trolling over her casting in Maa Behen after a promotional clip went viral. The episode has renewed debate over how digital creators are viewed when they enter mainstream cinema.

Janhvi Kapoor says Bollywood follows Excel sheets, Telugu cinema follows passion Janhvi Kapoor said Telugu cinema prioritises creativity over deadlines and praised the industry's work culture, while Ram Charan admired Bollywood's ability to finish films faster.

The Four Seasons 2 review: A moving reminder that friendship needs boundaries tooRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 In the messy aftermath of loss, The Four Seasons Season 2 finds beauty in letting go — proving that even the strongest bonds sometimes heal best with a little distance.

Shiva Rajkumar hopes Ram Charan wins National Award for Peddi: Praying for that Shiva Rajkumar has backed Ram Charan's performance in Peddi, saying he hopes the actor wins a National Award, just as Allu Arjun did for Pushpa: The Rise.

The Great Grand Superhero director says film explores rare side of Jackie Shroff Director Manish Saini praised Jackie Shroff's nuanced performance in The Great Grand Superhero, saying the actor revealed a side audiences have rarely seen before. The filmmaker also spoke about making films purely for joy, rejecting creative pressure and conventional definitions of success.

Alia Bhatt's Alpha gets new release date, arrives a week earlier than planned Alpha, starring Alia Bhatt and Sharvari Wagh, has been moved from July 10 to July 3. The shift gives the YRF Spy Universe film a clearer run after Dhamaal 4 changed its date.

general news.headline: You'd be in prison if not for me: Trump blasts Netanyahu over Lebanon escalationUS President Donald Trump reportedly confronted Benjamin Netanyahu in a tense call over Israel's strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon. The exchange exposed strain between US diplomacy with Iran and Israel's stated military course.

Can Mamata save her TMC, or will it slip out of her hands?The Trinamool Congress is facing an existential crisis with several leaders having left or on the verge of quitting. Will TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee be able to fight the desertions and planned defections, and rebuild the party, or will it slip away from her hands?

Four days late, then login errors: CBSE re-evaluation portal faces fresh troubleAfter four days of delay and revised schedules, CBSE's re-evaluation portal went live on June 2 before students reported fresh access issues, adding to concerns around answer-sheet verification and admissions.

Monsoon's Kerala date postponed again: IMD model predicts new timelineThe IMD's latest weather model indicates that a strong monsoon may develop only after June 5-6, suggesting a weaker onset over Kerala before rainfall gradually intensifies.

If Ganga-Jamuni tehzeeb was harmony, why does Kashi's Nandi still wait?For centuries, the Nandi has kept his silent vigil in Kashi, gazing towards the spot where the sanctum once stood, even as generations were taught to call it a fountain. The idea of Ganga-Jamuni tehzeeb is celebrated as a story of harmony and synthesis. If it truly is a confluence of rivers, why does Nandi still wait for his Shiva?

30 Indian truck drivers, arrested for living illegally in US, to be deportedUS Border Patrol agents in Arizona's Yuma Sector arrested 52 undocumented individuals during Operation Checkmate between May 11 and 15, including 36 who were working as semi-truck drivers.

3,400 deaths in a day: India's extreme heat days are turning deadlierOfficial counts of "heatstroke deaths" are often low, sometimes just a few hundred in a bad season, because many heat-related deaths are not labelled as such. This implies that the toll could be much higher.

Parliament panel summons Education Secretary, CBSE chief as OSM row intensifiesThe Parliament Standing Committee has summoned education officials, CERT-In and student representatives to examine complaints over CBSE's Class 12 on-screen marking system, including alleged glitches, evaluation discrepancies and post-result verification issues.

Was like a kept boyfriend: Lalit Modi defends Sushmita Sen over gold digger tagLalit Modi has defended Sushmita Sen against the 'gold digger' label that followed their 2022 relationship reveal. He said she was self-made and never depended on him.

TMC suspends 2 MLAs after Suvendu Adhikari's signature forgery claimThe expulsion order came immediately after Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said in his press conference that the two expelled MLAs had filed complaints of signature forgery in a party letter that endorsed Sovondeb Chattopadhay as the Leader of the Opposition in the state assembly.

2 friends, 1 hostel and the 1-mark difference: The story of JEE Advanced AIR 1 and AIR 2Shubham Kumar and Kabir Chhillar, friends from the same Kota hostel, secured AIR 1 and AIR 2 in JEE Advanced 2026. Their result turned a one-mark gap into a story of friendship, discipline and shared ambition.

Do judges need so many breaks? A look at an SC judge's day to settle the debateThe Supreme Court has entered a six-week summer recess with only the vacation benches hearing urgent cases. The break has kickstarted the annual ritualistic debate over judicial vacations amid high pendency. Here's a look at an SC judge's day to settle the debate.

No India-Pak mission in Alpha, Alia Bhatt's film is an assassin origin storyAlia Bhatt's Alpha is being positioned as an assassin origin story in the YRF Spy Universe, not an India-Pakistan joint mission. The shift moves the film away from rumours about a possible mission involving two countries and towards a harder action-led narrative.

After warning of AI job losses, Sam Altman now says AI is creating jobsSam Altman said companies adopting AI most aggressively are also hiring the most. His remarks add to a more cautious debate over whether AI is truly driving layoffs or being used to explain them.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

Anthropic files for IPO, may become $1 trillion publicly listed companyJust days after overtaking OpenAI in valuation, Anthropic has taken another major step in the AI race by confidentially filing for an IPO. The move could value the Claude maker at nearly $1 trillion, potentially making it one of the most valuable publicly listed companies in the world.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget herRajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Bengaluru father's dance with daughter in baby carrier leaves 22 million smilingAn adorable video of a Bengaluru man dancing with his toddler daughter in a baby carrier might just be the pick-me-up you need.

Live: CBSE opens portal for marks verification and re-evaluationCBSE Class 12 Re-evaluation 2026 Live: Students reported fresh glitches on the CBSE Class 12 re-evaluation portal, alleging login failures, frozen screens, unchecked answers and incorrectly assessed MCQs, even as the board's post-result services process went live after repeated delays.

Why Abhishek, and not Mamata Banerjee, is facing the most anger in West BengalPeople are mocking Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee and have brought protests to his doorsteps. Here's why the anger, even from TMC leaders, seems mostly directed at bhaipo (nephew), and not at party chief Mamata Banerjee.

Off Campus and Heated Rivalry have sparked a 'hockey butt' conversationThanks to Off Campus and Heated Rivalry, the hockey butt is having a moment online. But beyond the viral fascination lies a bigger story about strength, mobility, and ageing well.

45-litre tank, 52 litres of petrol: Kanpur car owner alleges fuel pump scamA car owner in Uttar Pradesh's Kanpur has claimed that a local petrol pump billed him for 52 litres of petrol while topping up a car with a 45-litre tank. District officials have begun a probe into the man's allegations.

Ladakh talks hit snag as Sonam Wangchuk alleges key points omitted from draftWangchuk warned omissions in the draft minutes could erode trust from the talks, while Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra said the draft was provisional and any omissions could be addressed.

Difficult but not impossible: IAEA chief on moving Iran's enriched uraniumIAEA chief Rafael Grossi said transferring Iran's highly enriched uranium remains possible despite technical challenges. The remarks highlighted a central sticking point as Tehran and Washington pursue a broader nuclear understanding.

sports.headline: PV Sindhu wins Indonesia Open opener, potential An Se-young clash ahead PV Sindhu opened her Indonesia Open campaign with a straight-games win over Busanan Ongbamrungphan. The result sends her into the next round, where world No. 1 An Se-young is the likely challenge.

Franchise cricket's boom forces ICC to take action on jam-packed calendar The ICC has set up a committee to examine how the surge of franchise leagues can coexist with the international calendar. The move reflects growing concern that bilateral cricket is losing ground as players and boards follow the T20 money.

Arnaldi outlasts Tiafoe in 5-hour epic to seal historic Italian trio in Paris Roland Garros: Matteo Arnaldi outlasted Frances Tiafoe in a five-set French Open epic to reach the quarter-finals. The comeback completed a rare Italian trio in the last eight and set up a meeting with Matteo Berrettini.

Sabalenka and Osaka unite after Roland Garros clash, want women in prime-time slots Roland Garros 2026: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka formed a united front at French Open, demanding more prime-time visibility for women's tennis after their historic fourth-round night clash ended a three-year shutout.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget her Rajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Sabalenka aces Michael Jackson's moonwalk after beating Osaka: Video Aryna Sabalenka beat Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-3 to reach the French Open quarter-finals before breaking into a moonwalk on Court Philippe-Chatrier. The celebratory flourish capped another commanding display from the World No. 1.

Contract crisis hits ISL as futures of nearly 150 players hang in the balance Nearly 150 ISL players became free agents after their contracts expired amid the AIFF-club stand-off over next season. The ongoing uncertainty has weakened players' bargaining power and hampered clubs' preparations for the upcoming campaign.

Berrettini avenges Jannik Sinner, knocks Cerundolo out in straight sets Italian Matteo Berrettini avenged his compatriot Jannik Sinner in the French Open after beating young Juan Manuel Cerundolo on Monday, June 1. Cerundolo's impressive run in the tournament was stopped in the fourth round.

World Cup: South Africa squad departs without assistant coach after US visa delay South Africa's World Cup squad departed for Mexico on Monday, 24 hours later than scheduled, after visa complications disrupted the team's travel plans and left assistant coach Helman Mkhalele stranded in Johannesburg.

Thomas Tuchel backs England to overcome heat and humidity at FIFA World Cup England manager Thomas Tuchel has backed his side to cope with the heat and humidity expected at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, insisting the challenging conditions in North America will be an obstacle to overcome rather than an excuse for underperformance.

Mischievous RCB stars pour ice-cold water on coach after IPL win Royal Challengers Bengaluru celebrated their IPL win in style at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. RCB players Tim David and Salt emptied the entire ice bucket on head coach Andy Flower after the final.

Tim David suspended for RCB's IPL 2027 opener after ice-bag incident with umpireRoyal Challengers Bengaluru batter Tim David has been suspended for the franchise's opening match of IPL 2027 after breaching the league's Code of Conduct during the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Serena Williams confirms shock tennis comeback at 44 for Queen's ChampionshipsSerena Williams has confirmed her return to professional tennis at the age of 44, nearly four years after her last competitive appearance. Williams has accepted a doubles' wildcard at the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club in London.

Balaji-Demoliner reach doubles quarters, Arnav Paparkar enters boys' last 16N. Sriram Balaji and Brazil's Marcelo Demoliner advanced to the men's doubles quarterfinals of the French Open on Monday, while Arnav Paparkar continued India's strong showing by reaching the boys' singles round of 16 in the junior competition.

Don't need to prove it to anybody: Satwik-Chirag just want Badminton to flourishSingapore Open 2026: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty ended their two-year title wait by winning the Singapore Open. Their response afterwards was pointed: the win settled nothing for them, but renewed a call for sport to be recognised and supported properly.

French Open fines player Rs 71 lakh for sexist remark on refereeParaguayan tennis player Adolfo Vallejo has been fined 65,000 euros by the French Open after making sexist remarks about chair umpire Ana Carvalho. The controversy stems from his dramatic second-round defeat at Roland Garros.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A preview: Can Mexico finally end their quarterfinal jinx?FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A Preview: The stage is finally set for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with Group A kicking off things. Mexico start as the hot favourites but South Korea, Czechia and South Africa will be aiming to disrupt the party for the co-hosts.

ICC approves trial use of pink ball in Tests hit by bad lightThe usage of pink balls on a trial basis during Test matches in case bad light affects play, and allowing head coaches inside the field of play during drink breaks, are among the major changes to the playing conditions approved by the International Cricket Council (ICC) Board at its annual meeting in Ahmedabad.

ICC suspends Cricket Canada, national teams remain eligible for eventsThe International Cricket Council (ICC) suspended Cricket Canada with immediate effect for serious breaches of its membership obligations following the conclusion of its Board meetings in Ahmedabad.

Carlos Alcaraz enjoys beach vacation in Portugal amid injury layoff periodCarlos Alcaraz was spotted enjoying a beach getaway in Portugal while recovering from the wrist injury that has kept him out of major tournaments, including Roland Garros and Wimbledon.

RCB's dressing room is the biggest prize, not the IPLAndy Flower's tribute to Virat Kohli, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood offered a glimpse into the culture behind RCB's back-to-back IPL titles, where leadership, trust and selflessness matter as much as talent.

PV Sindhu congratulates RCB on IPL glory, adds cheeky lucky charm remarkPV Sindhu congratulated Royal Challengers Bengaluru after their successful IPL title defence and joked that her move to Bangalore has coincided with their winning run. On Sunday, RCB beat Gujarat Titans in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium.

Zee to broadcast FIFA World Cup 2026 in India, secures 8-year dealFIFA World Cup 2026: India was one of the last major markets without a World Cup broadcaster. That changed on Monday as Zee secured rights to the 2026 tournament and a host of FIFA events through 2034.

Senegal AFCON walkout sparks major law changes ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup: IFAB has approved sweeping law changes after AFCON 2026 flashpoints, including Senegal's final walkout and tougher sanctions for protest and confrontation. The measures will apply from 2026-27 and at World Cup 2026, signalling a harder line on dissent, delay and discriminatory conduct.

RCB's mane attraction: Rajat Patidar, like MS Dhoni is making calm the new cool againIPL 2026: The long hair may invite comparisons to MS Dhoni, but Rajat Patidar's calm leadership, trust in players and ability to stay composed under pressure are what truly make RCB's captain resemble Captain Cool.

RCB unlikely to hold victory parade in Bengaluru after IPL title winRCB fans are unlikely to get a victory parade after the team's IPL title defence, with the franchise opting against public celebrations in Bengaluru amid safety concerns and strict police guidelines following last year's deadly stampede.

Secretly been working with Sat-Chi: Ex-Danish shuttler after Singapore Open winMathias Boe has revealed he quietly rejoined Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty as a consultant before their Singapore Open win. The move restores a proven tactical alliance as the pair build towards the world championships in Delhi and the Asian Games in Japan.

Rajat Patidar not done yet, wants RCB to script IPL hat-trick historyRajat Patidar said he wants to help Royal Challengers Bengaluru achieve an unprecedented hat-trick of IPL titles. On Sunday, RCB became only the third franchise in tournament history to win back-to-back championships, but no team has ever gone on to win three titles in a row.

Luis Suarez left out of Uruguay World Cup squad as Valverde, Nunez make cutFIFA World Cup: Marcelo Bielsa left Luis Suarez and Nahitan Nandez out of Uruguay's final 26-man World Cup squad. The decision reinforces his youth-first, high-intensity reset despite Suarez's attempted return and recent criticism.

I'll remember IPL 2026 for Sooryavanshi: Sunil Gavaskar after RCB vs GT finalSunil Gavaskar said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's six-hitting and powerplay run surge defined IPL 2026 for him. The praise underlined how a 15-year-old's fearless season has shifted the conversation to Indian cricket's next batting force.

Day 9 Schedule: Aryna Sabalenka, Naomi Osaka face off in blockbuster clashFrench Open 2026 Day 9: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka are set to square off in a blockbuster fourth-round clash during the night session, while India's N. Sriram Balaji, Maaya Rajeshwaran Revathi and Aryan Paparkar will also be in action at Roland-Garros.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
